title: Garlicky Indulgent Tunisian Marzipan Tagine
description: A rich and aromatic tagine made with marzipan, garlic, and spices, perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over low heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it is lightly caramelized.
Add the ground ginger, cinnamon, cumin, and salt to the saucepan and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the almond paste (marzipan) to the saucepan and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until it is lightly toasted.
Add the granulated sugar to the saucepan and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until it is well combined with the almond paste.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the heavy cream and confectioners' sugar until stiff peaks form.
Add the rose water to the cream mixture and whisk until well combined.
Pour the cream mixture over the saucepan mixture and stir until well combined.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over low heat and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it has thickened slightly.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Transfer the mixture to a serving dish or individual ramekins and chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Just before serving, sprinkle the chopped pistachios over the top of the tagine.
